response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, testing, and craftsmanship credentials, but the role demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. Brief exposure to low-latency systems (DLang at Sociomantic) and C/C++ tooling (National Instruments) is tangential at best, and there is no evidence of GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload optimization experience.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O and fibers)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical teaching
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or silicon/hardware architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference or GPU workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level performance architecture for AI accelerators

key_factors.factor: General performance and low-latency systems background
